• Installed pypsa, pandas, numpy, matplotlib, seaborn and their
• Verified the workspace structure and respected the read-only constraints
• Built a pypsa.Network from the CSV inputs:
• buses.csv → adds 20 buses (400 kV, GB-like coordinates).
• links.csv → adds AC transmission links with specified capacities and
• generators.csv → adds onshore wind, gas, and nuclear generators with
• storage.csv → adds three pumped-hydro storage units as StorageUnits
• demand.csv → adds time-dependent nodal loads for 168 hourly snapshots
• wind_cf.csv → sets time-varying p_max_pu for wind generators based on
• Solved the linear optimisation via n.optimize(solver_name="highs").
• Exported (where available) main result tables to outputs/:
Note: HiGHS/linopy reports the LP as infeasible for the given dataset and
constraints (no load shedding or slack generator).
• report/images/demand_total.png: total system demand over the 168 hours.
• report/images/wind_cf_avg.png: average wind capacity factor over time
• Script attempts to read outputs/generators_p.csv, group generators by
carrier (based on name prefix), and plot stacked area generation by
• Because the optimisation is infeasible, generators_p.csv contains no
numeric dispatch columns (only the snapshot index), which leads to “no
numeric data to plot”. This matches the infeasibility diagnosis.
• HiGHS reports “Problem status: Infeasible” at presolve.
• Very high nodal demand (several GW per bus) vs. available firm capacity
• Periods of low wind capacity factors while demand remains high.
• Limited PHS storage power/energy capacity relative to system load.
• Possible network congestion limiting transfers from generation-rich to
• A standard remedy in research models would be to add:
• High-cost slack generators at each bus, so that an optimal solution
always exists and unmet load is priced explicitly.
• Discussion of solver infeasibility and what it implies.
• Limitations and recommended extensions (load shedding, longer horizons,
CO₂ constraints, additional flexibility, extension to 29-node FES-style
• Reproducibility instructions (how to rerun model.py and analysis.py).
